title: Support for Image Signing
body: Does podman support image signing from the CLI similar to docker-ce/notary? If not, is it on the roadmap?

comments.body: I believe @QiWang19 is starting to work on signing support in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1899 but for the most part we recommend you sign images using `skopeo`
We support Simple SIgning right now, no Notary support.  We are open to Support for Notary, but are not currently working on it.

There already is `podman push --sign-by=`, and `podman pull` does, AFAIK, verify signatures according to the systemwide signature policy.

So, the primary elements of “simple signing” are already available in `podman`; the above-linked `podman trust` work can help with setting up the configuration but is not, strictly speaking, necessary to benefit on push/pull.
I think that TUF support should be put on the roadmap since quay.io supports it
@rhatdan @QiWang19 are we at a point now where we can close this?
